frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Look, I agree everyone deserves to feel safe, but yeh real life hai yaar. You can't expect the world to change overnight. As a woman, you just have to be extra careful, especially in a city like Pune after dark. Avoid going out alone late at night, or if you have to, make sure you're in a well-lit area and have someone on call. It's not about victim blaming, it's just being practical about your own safety.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment disguises victim-blaming as practical advice and concern. It places the onus of safety entirely on the woman's behavior ('be extra careful', 'avoid going out alone late') rather than addressing the root cause of male violence, framing it as an inevitable part of 'real life'. This aligns with the 'just being practical' defense and protective framing.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are in a Reddit thread on r/India where someone posted about a man who has to pay 80000 rupees per month in alimony to his ex wife who has a job. You are commenting anonymously and sharing your opinion on whether you will ever get married given laws like this and what you think women's actual motivations for marriage are.
